query: As of today (July 1, 2026), is OpenAI's GPT-5.6 generally available, and is it scored/ranked on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index leaderboard (artificialanalysis.ai / DataLearner mirror), or is GPT-5.6 still only a government-gated limited preview announced June 26, 2026? Distinguish clearly between GPT-5.5 (released April 2026, scored on the leaderboard) and GPT-5.6 (limited preview). What is OpenAI's actual highest-scoring generally-available model on the AA Intelligence Index right now, and its score/rank?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is the summary of findings:

## Answer

**GPT-5.6 is NOT generally available as of July 1, 2026.** It remains a **government-gated limited preview** announced Friday, June 26, 2026 — released only to ~20 U.S.-government-vetted "trusted partners" under the Trump administration's AI executive order. It is **NOT scored or ranked** on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (nor the DataLearner/BenchLM mirror).

**Clear distinction between the two models:**

| | GPT-5.5 | GPT-5.6 (Sol/Terra/Luna) |
|---|---|---|
| Released | April 23, 2026 | Previewed June 26, 2026 |
| Availability | Generally available | Limited preview (~20 gov-approved partners) |
| On AA Index? | **Yes** (scored & ranked) | **No** (absent) |
| AA Index score | **55 (xhigh), rank #3** | Not scored |

**OpenAI's highest-scoring generally-available model on the AA Intelligence Index right now is GPT-5.5 (xhigh): score 55, rank #3 overall** — behind Claude Fable 5 (60) and Claude Opus 4.8 (56). GPT-5.5 was #1 at its April launch but has since been surpassed by newer Anthropic models.

OpenAI plans broad GA for GPT-5.6 "in the coming weeks" (Axios cited mid-July as best case), but that had not happened as of July 1, 2026, and no GPT-5.6 variant appears on any public AA Intelligence Index snapshot.

key_facts: GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) was announced as a limited preview on June 26, 2026, and is NOT generally available as of July 1, 2026.
GPT-5.6 is restricted to roughly 20 U.S.-government-approved 'trusted partners' at the Trump administration's request under a June 2, 2026 AI executive order.
GPT-5.6 is NOT scored or ranked on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index leaderboard (nor on the DataLearner/BenchLM mirror).
GPT-5.5 was released April 23, 2026, is generally available, and IS scored on the AA Intelligence Index.
OpenAI's highest-scoring generally-available model on the AA Intelligence Index is GPT-5.5 (xhigh) with a score of 55, ranked #3 overall.
The current AA Intelligence Index top models are Claude Fable 5 (with fallback) at 60, Claude Opus 4.8 (max) at 56, then GPT-5.5 (xhigh) at 55.
GPT-5.5 was #1 on the AA Intelligence Index when it launched in April 2026 but has since been surpassed by newer Anthropic releases.
GPT-5.6 is the first frontier model released by an American company under a government-managed customer-by-customer access list.
OpenAI plans broad GA of GPT-5.6 'in the coming weeks' (Axios cited mid-July as best case), but broad release had not occurred as of July 1, 2026.
GPT-5.6 planned pricing when GA: Sol $5/M input and $30/M output; Terra ~half of Sol; Luna $1/M input and $6/M output.
